statements.impact_statement: Transmute does not use progs/infocmp.c, function analyze_string(), which is the vulnerable code path. The vulnerable code is not in the execute path of Transmute, and cannot be reached by an attacker.

Transmute does not use Perl Archive::Tar to extract untrusted tar archives. Its tar extraction path is implemented in Python via backend/converters/archive_convert.py and uses tarfile.extractall(path=dest, filter='data'), which rejects symlinks and hardlinks that escape the extraction directory. The vulnerable Archive::Tar hardlink extraction code path is not in the execute path of Transmute and cannot be reached by an attacker through the application.

Transmute does not use Perl Archive::Tar to extract untrusted tar archives. Its tar extraction path is implemented in Python via backend/converters/archive_convert.py and uses tarfile.extractall(path=dest, filter='data'), which rejects symlinks and hardlinks that escape the extraction directory. The vulnerable Archive::Tar symlink extraction code path is not in the execute path of Transmute and cannot be reached by an attacker through the application.

Transmute does not include the vulnerable Perl IO::Compress component in its container image, and the application does not use Perl File::GlobMapper or attacker-controlled output globs. Inspection of the built image shows no installed IO::Compress module in Perl's @INC and no Debian IO::Compress package such as libio-compress-perl in the package inventory, so the vulnerable component is not present in the product.

Transmute does not use Perl Archive::Tar to parse or extract untrusted tar archives. Its tar-handling path is implemented in Python via backend/converters/archive_convert.py and uses Python tarfile rather than Perl Archive::Tar. The vulnerable Archive::Tar _read_tar() memory-exhaustion path is not in the execute path of Transmute and cannot be reached by an attacker through the application.

Transmute does not use Perl HTTP::Daemon or its send_file() method. Its HTTP download path is implemented in Python in backend/downloaders/http_downloader.py using httpx, and the application does not expose attacker-controlled input to Perl HTTP::Daemon::send_file(). The vulnerable HTTP::Daemon send_file() command-injection path is not in the execute path of Transmute and cannot be reached by an attacker through the application.
